extension_name: Devinization Pack 5 — Quality, Standards & Human Review

included_families.family_name: Approval UX, Review Queues & Human Control

purpose: Define the quality-control layer that prevents the Station Chief runtime and future agent workforce from shipping sloppy outputs, stale information, weak evidence, missing review gates, bad templates, unclear approvals, unverified claims, incomplete deliverables, or good enough work that is not actually usable.
core_operating_philosophy: The Agent Command Center remains a massive command civilization with selective live deployment. The whole 175-family agency remains available for awareness, routing, governance, memory, standards, review, and quality control. This pack does not shrink the art piece. This pack defines how work becomes trustworthy, reviewable, standards-compliant, evidence-backed, approval-ready, and actually useful. Created is not quality checked, and human-control boundaries remain explicit.
activation_rule: Use this pack when the user says or implies: quality check, review this, standards, template, benchmark, evaluate, compliance, evidence, source freshness, citation check, human review, approval queue, final review, deliverable standard, is this good enough, make it professional, make it usable, check for stale info, verify sources, risk review, QA gate, approval needed, ready to ship, client-ready, portfolio-ready, regulated deliverable, attestation, no fake quality, no sloppy output.

quality_review_layers.name: Quality Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Check whether the work satisfies the objective, avoids obvious defects, and meets the expected standard before review.

quality_review_layers.name: Standards & Template Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Ensure outputs follow the correct format, template, naming convention, structure, tone, and methodology.

quality_review_layers.name: Evidence & Source Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Verify that factual claims, citations, source freshness, proof artifacts, and regulated evidence are adequate.

quality_review_layers.name: Risk & Compliance Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Identify compliance, safety, legal, privacy, reputation, and business risks before delivery.

quality_review_layers.name: Human Review Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Route work to human review when judgment, approval, sensitive action, or final signoff is required.

quality_review_layers.name: Benchmark & Evaluation Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Compare outputs against rubrics, benchmarks, prior versions, known-good examples, and measurable criteria.

quality_review_layers.name: Deliverable Governance Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Confirm the work product is complete, usable, labeled, versioned, and ready for the intended audience.

quality_review_layers.name: Approval UX Gate
quality_review_layers.purpose: Present review decisions, approval choices, change requests, and final control points clearly to the human operator.

global_rules: 1. Preserve the locked 175-family baseline unless the user explicitly authorizes baseline mutation.
2. Use overlays for Devinization unless explicitly instructed otherwise.
3. Do not treat created as quality checked.
4. Quality review must check objective fit, completeness, accuracy, formatting, evidence, and usability.
5. Standards must be explicit before judging compliance.
6. Templates are contracts when strict execution mode is active.
7. Human review is required for risky, regulated, irreversible, sensitive, or ambiguous final actions.
8. Source freshness must be checked for time-sensitive facts.
9. Evidence must be attached or cited when claims require proof.
10. Deliverables must be audience-ready, not just internally complete.
11. Approval queues must show what needs review, what passed, what failed, and what is blocked.
12. Review comments must be specific, actionable, and tied to the exact issue.
13. Benchmarks must compare against a defined rubric or known-good example.
14. Compliance evidence must preserve source, date, scope, and attestation notes.
15. Do not bury critical quality failures under mostly done.
16. Ready to ship requires quality gate, scope gate, evidence gate, and human-control gate when applicable.
17. Preserve the art piece: quality controls protect the full agency without shrinking the command civilization.

crews.mission: Check whether work satisfies the objective, meets expected quality, avoids obvious defects, and is ready for deeper review.
Identify operational, compliance, privacy, legal, reputation, workflow, and user-trust risks in a task or deliverable.
Screen outputs for compliance issues, regulated claims, missing disclaimers, evidence gaps, and audience-sensitive delivery concerns.
Review factual accuracy, internal consistency, source support, and whether claims are framed with the correct level of certainty.
Convert quality failures into precise repair instructions, preserving valid work and fixing only the defective areas.
Decide whether a work product is ready for final delivery, human review, revision, or blocking.
Define evaluation rubrics for outputs, prompts, runtime behavior, deliverables, reports, and agent performance.
Compare outputs against known-good examples, prior versions, locked standards, templates, or expected performance levels.
Check whether new outputs or system changes reduced quality, broke prior standards, or drifted from locked behavior.
Preserve scoring evidence, evaluation notes, benchmark references, pass/fail reasons, and review artifacts.
Evaluate agent behavior for usefulness, scope discipline, output quality, tool efficiency, and ability to follow Devin-specific operating rules.
Convert failed evaluations into precise corrective action, benchmark changes, prompt repairs, or human review routing.
Route tasks, outputs, external actions, and deliverables to human review when judgment, sensitivity, approval, or risk requires it.
Assemble review packets with objective, output, changes, evidence, risks, unresolved questions, and recommended decision.
Capture human feedback, approvals, requested changes, objections, corrections, and final decision notes.
Escalate unclear, high-risk, disputed, or blocked review items to the correct governance or approval layer.
Define what the system may approve automatically and what must remain under human control.
Close review loops by recording decisions, final changes, unresolved items, and whether the deliverable is approved, blocked, or revision-ready.
Maintain standards for format, naming, tone, structure, evidence, versioning, output type, and project-specific methodology.
Treat templates as contracts when required, preserving section order, labels, placeholders, and exact user-specified format.
Choose the correct methodology for the task: audit, build, repair, research, validation, documentation, report, or prompt-writing.
Check whether the chosen method fits the task and whether the system skipped necessary steps.
Verify outputs match required templates, formatting rules, table structures, headings, naming conventions, and exact prompt/report patterns.
Identify reusable standards, templates, rubrics, validation patterns, review forms, and delivery formats for future work.
Define what evidence is required for claims, compliance-sensitive content, regulated deliverables, and final reports.
Collect or reference evidence artifacts, citations, validator output, screenshots, source files, commit hashes, and proof notes.
Create attestation notes that state what was checked, what evidence supports it, what remains uncertain, and what the system can honestly claim.
Review regulated, insurance, financial, legal, medical, safety, or compliance-sensitive wording for unsupported claims or risky phrasing.
Add or recommend disclaimers, scope notes, uncertainty statements, review-needed flags, and audience-appropriate boundaries.
Review deliverables that may be used externally, professionally, commercially, or in regulated settings before they are marked ready.
Determine whether information requires fresh verification and flag stale, time-sensitive, or source-dependent claims.
Track whether knowledge is current, outdated, uncertain, evergreen, or requires online/source verification before use.
Verify that citations, file references, source notes, or evidence links actually support the claims they are attached to.
Assign confidence levels to claims based on evidence strength, source quality, freshness, and uncertainty.
Identify missing sources, weak sources, stale references, unsupported details, and facts that require verification before delivery.
Record when knowledge was verified, what source supported it, and when it should be rechecked.
Check whether a deliverable is complete, usable, audience-ready, labeled, formatted, versioned, and supported by proof.
Ensure files, reports, packs, artifacts, and deliverables use clear versioning, labels, commit references, and naming conventions.
Define how deliverables should be packaged for internal use, client use, portfolio use, handoff, or future runtime integration.
Feed review results, failure patterns, approval outcomes, and standards changes back into future deliverable governance.
Archive completed deliverables, review records, proof artifacts, version notes, and approval records for future reuse and audit.
Identify defects in deliverables such as missing sections, broken formatting, weak explanation, unsupported claims, wrong audience fit, or unusable structure.

crews.family_anchor: 131. Approval UX, Review Queues & Human Control
crews.mission: Add work items to review queues with context, reason, priority, evidence, risk level, and requested human decision.

crews.family_anchor: 131. Approval UX, Review Queues & Human Control
crews.mission: Flag approvals that involve risk, external action, regulated output, baseline mutation, public release, or irreversible change.

crews.family_anchor: 131. Approval UX, Review Queues & Human Control
crews.mission: Present approval choices clearly: approve, reject, revise, block, escalate, archive, or request more evidence.

crews.family_anchor: 131. Approval UX, Review Queues & Human Control
crews.mission: Record approval history, reviewer notes, decision rationale, artifacts reviewed, and whether the decision is final or conditional.

crews.family_anchor: 131. Approval UX, Review Queues & Human Control
crews.mission: Convert human review feedback into specific, scoped, testable change requests with owner, affected area, and success criteria.

crews.family_anchor: 131. Approval UX, Review Queues & Human Control
crews.mission: Lock human-control boundaries so sensitive, risky, irreversible, external, or high-impact decisions cannot be silently automated.
